chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Present Mr. Justice Adnan Igbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Muhammad Jaffer Raza Constitution Petition No. D – 4889 of 2025 (Abdul Ali & others Vs. Federation of Pakistan & others) Petitioners: Abdul Ali, Harun-u-Rashid, Joynal Matubber, Any Akter accompanied by children namely (MD Rohan, MD Khizer & Muhammad Ayub), Abdul Jabbar Bepari, Shamsul Haque, Kazi Fazal Hoque, MD. Shahjahan Mia, Saiful Islam, Noor Hussain, Shar Bano, Noori Akhtar, Noor Islam, Noor Alam, Nizamuddin, Nasiruddin, Mofiz Ahmed, Paher Ahmed Biplob, Shohidullah and Kamran Shikder through Mr. Raamiz Nasim Advocate. Respondents 1 to 6: Federation of Pakistan, Inspector General of Police, Director FIA Sindh, DSP FRO, Addl. Deputy Director FIA and Home Secretary, Home Department through Ms. Mehreen Ibrahim DAG along with DSP Ayaz Ahmed Paidhiani, Foreign Registration Office Special Branch Karachi, SIP Jawaid Akhtar and Lady SIP Mehvish Iftikhar, FIA. Date of hearing: 29.10.2025 Date of decision: 29.10.2025 ORDER Muhammad Jaffer Raza, J – Learned DAG files para-wise comments on behalf of respondents No.2 & 4, the same are taken on record, copies whereof have been provided to the learned counsel appearing for the petitioners. It is evident from the comments furnished that the noted respondents have no objection to the grant of the instant petition subject to certain formalities enumerated therein. By consent, instant petition is disposed of in the following terms:- 1) The petitioners shall obtain travel permits from the High Commission of Bangladesh, Karachi. The same shall be verified from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for confirmation of their genuineness. 2) Petitioners are allowed to travel subject to further verification of their status, criminal record, CRO/CRI.

By consent, instant petition is disposed of in the following terms:- 1) The petitioners shall obtain travel permits from the High Commission of Bangladesh, Karachi. The same shall be verified from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for confirmation of their genuineness. 2) Petitioners are allowed to travel subject to further verification of their status, criminal record, CRO/CRI. 3) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is directed to verify the identity of the petitioners through biometric for NADRA registration. If the petitioners are registered in NADRA their CNICs may be cancelled/blocked. 4) The Director General, NADRA Sindh is further directed to provide certificate to the petitioners subject to the above. 5) After completion of all codal formalities as required under the Foreign Act, 1946, the Petitioners may be permitted to travel to their native country Bangladesh. J U D G E J U D G E *Nadeem Qureshi PA*
